messages.content:
HOW YOUR CLAIM BECOMES A NUMBER (the perturbation contract):
- You never state € impacts. You emit machine-readable ops; the engine computes
  ΔTAM/ΔSAM/ΔYAM by re-running the real funnel. Any € figure you write in prose
  will be stripped.
- Ops: {nodeId, op: "set"|"scale"|"add", value} rewrite one leaf's value;
  {nodeId, op: "exclude"} removes a dimension cell from the venture's reachable
  scope ("unavailable to us" — foreclosure), which is different from shrinking it.
- Ops apply in array order. Ratio nodes must stay inside [0,1]; tamBase must stay > 0.
- Only FUNNEL-ACTIVE nodes may be perturbed (marked in the ledger you receive).
  shape.cagr and shape.cr3 are context — a demand-pace or concentration risk must
  be translated into the funnel nodes it would actually move (tamBase level,
  serviceableFactor reach, obtainableFactor pace, or a share).
- Sibling shares are NOT re-normalized when you move one share (documented v1
  simplification). If you mean a mix-SHIFT, perturb multiple siblings; if you
  mean "this slice is smaller in absolute terms", one op is correct.
- Perturbing an assumption leaf overrides whatever the user set: your claim is
  about the world, not about their slider.

Fix the hypothesis's targetNodes/perturbation so it passes validation WITHOUT changing the claim's meaning. If the claim cannot be expressed against funnel-active nodes, translate it into the funnel nodes it would actually move.

FUNNEL-ACTIVE NODES:
tamBase (EUR_M, value 300)
geo.DE (ratio, value 0.5)
geo.NL (ratio, value 0.28)
geo.PL (ratio, value 0.07)
geo.CH (ratio, value 0.08)
geo.CZ (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.AT (ratio, value 0.02)
geo.other (ratio, value 0.03)
seg.hyperscale (ratio, value 0.44)
seg.colocation (ratio, value 0.18)
seg.enterprise (ratio, value 0.29)
seg.edge (ratio, value 0.05)
seg.telecom (ratio, value 0.04)
cust.operator-large (ratio, value 0.4)
cust.operator-mid (ratio, value 0.25)
cust.oem (ratio, value 0.22)
cust.distributor (ratio, value 0.13)
serviceableFactor (ratio, value 0.55)
obtainableFactor (ratio, value 0.01)

HYPOTHESIS:
{
 "id": "prelet-lockout-year1-contestability",
 "title": "83% pre-let pipeline plus long-lead electrical frameworks leave almost no contestable Year-1 PDU orders",
 "narrative": "The obtainableFactor's own cross-check assumes DC electrical gear is locked 12–24 months before fit-out, which keeps a ≤2% Year-1 share plausible. But the FLAP-D development pipeline is 83% pre-let at a record-low 6.3% vacancy — the 2026–27 builds a new entrant would sell into had their electrical bill-of-materials specified inside framework agreements signed at pre-let, i.e. in 2023–24. The venture's first 12 months open onto a market where the visible demand is real but already awarded: what remains contestable is churn and expansion inside operating halls, a fraction of the 1% anchor. The YAM is not conservative; it is counting orders that were signed before the company existed in the market.",
 "category": "execution",
 "targetNodes": [
  "obtainableFactor"
 ],
 "mechanism": [
  {
   "step": "trigger",
   "text": "FLAP-D vacancy hits a record-low 6.3% with the pipeline 83% pre-let — capacity is committed to named tenants years before commissioning, and pre-let deals carry integrated electrical packages fixed at design lock",
   "refs": [
    "digest:geo-DE#c19",
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "propagation",
   "text": "PDU line items ride those pre-let frameworks through the large-operator channel the venture targets first; RFPs that open during the venture's first 12 months are for capacity commissioning in 2028+, not for Year-1 revenue",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:cust.operator-large",
    "digest:geo-DE#c19",
    "profile:Who is the primary buyer you'll sell through first?"
   ]
  },
  {
   "step": "model-impact",
   "text": "The truly contestable Year-1 share of SAM sits below the 0.5% band floor; the 1% Prospeo anchor — already flagged as semantically fragile — overstates the first-year orderbook regardless of SAM size",
   "refs": [
    "ledger:obtainableFactor"
   ]
  }
 ],
 "whyMissable": "The 1% Year-1 benchmark reads as already-haircut — it is the bottom of the published 1–5% range, so it looks conservative. The pre-let statistic lives in commercial real-estate reports (JLL EMEA year-end), not in any PDU market report, so nobody on the team joins the two documents; the funnel has no 'already-awarded vs contestable' dimension where this could surface.",
 "expectedObviousness": "low",
 "falsifier": "Three or more CE colocation fit-outs commissioning in 2026–27 running open rack-PDU bake-offs (multi-bidder, no incumbent framework) during the venture's first two quarters.",
 "likelihood": {
  "value": 0.45,
  "rationale": "The 83% pre-let rate and record-low vacancy are documented for exactly the venture's window; offsetting this, retrofit, expansion and mid-market colo demand remains genuinely open, so total foreclosure is unlikely.",
  "basis": "evidence"
 },
 "perturbation": [
  {
   "nodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "op": "set",
   "value": 0.004,
   "note": "Contestable Year-1 share falls below the current 0.5% band floor once pre-awarded fit-out demand is netted out"
  }
 ],
 "indicators": [
  {
   "signal": "Share of CE data-center electrical/PDU procurements that are open multi-bidder vs sole-source or framework call-offs",
   "where": "TED (EU tenders portal) and major operator procurement portals (Digital Realty, Equinix, NTT supplier registration)",
   "threshold": "<20% of PDU line items contested openly over two quarters",
   "updates": "increases"
  },
  {
   "signal": "FLAP-D pre-let percentage and vacancy in successive half-year prints",
   "where": "JLL EMEA Data Centre Report and Cushman & Wakefield EMEA updates",
   "threshold": "Pre-let stays above 80% with vacancy below 8%",
   "updates": "increases"
  }
 ],
 "mitigations": [
  {
   "action": "Map the 2026–27 CE commissioning pipeline project-by-project and audit whether each electrical package is already awarded or still open",
   "type": "information",
   "voiNodeId": "obtainableFactor",
   "note": "Converts the 0.5–3% band into a named-project contestable-demand figure"
  },
  {
   "action": "Re-aim the Year-1 motion at expansion and retrofit demand in operating halls and mid-market colo, where procurement is not pre-awarded at building pre-let",
   "type": "strategic",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  },
  {
   "action": "Pursue approved-vendor-list qualification inside at least one operator framework agreement so the venture is inside the wall when call-offs occur",
   "type": "contractual",
   "voiNodeId": null,
   "note": null
  }
 ],
 "lens": "demand-discontinuity",
 "riskId": "risk.demand-discontinuity.prelet-lockout-year1-contestability"
}

VALIDATION ERROR:
[
  {
    "code": "invalid_value",
    "values": [
      "error",
      "risk"
    ],
    "path": [
      0,
      "resolution"
    ],
    "message": "Invalid option: expected one of \"error\"|\"risk\""
  }
]

Emit the corrected hypothesis JSON (same shape).
